subject: Chemistry
title: How Batteries Store Energy
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: How Batteries Store Energy.

hook.text: A battery is a device that converts chemical energy directly into electrical energy through redox reactions.

buildup.text: Inside, two different metals sit in an electrolyte. One wants to lose electrons, the other wants to gain them.

discovery.text: Electrons flow from the anode to the cathode through an external circuit—that flow is electricity.

twist.text: Rechargeable batteries reverse the reaction. Plugging in forces electrons back, restoring the original chemicals.

climax.text: Lithium-ion batteries dominate because lithium ions shuttle between electrodes efficiently for thousands of cycles.

punchline.text: Chemicals swap electrons through a wire. That's a battery.

quiz.question: What produces electricity inside a battery?
options: Chemical redox reactions that push electrons through an external circuit
Tiny magnets spinning inside the casing
Heat generated by friction between metals
